name: Sierra de Gata: Jálama Loop
name_original: Cilleros y Trevejo desde Hoyos — circular por las Tres Reyas y Jálama
description: This difficult 46.5-mile mountain biking route from Hoyos explores the Sierra de Gata, featuring Pico Jálama and ancient paths.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Hoyos, located in the Sierra de Gata region of Cáceres, Spain, offers a diverse and rugged landscape for outdoor activities. The area is characterized by rolling hills, mountains like Mount Moncalvo (1055 m) and Pico Lameros (866 m), and extensive forests of oak and cork oak. Abundant water resources, including the Santa Catallina and Razas rivers, contribute to the natural environment. This low mountain territory provides varied terrain suitable for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Hoyos The most popular mountain bike trail is Trevejo desde Hoyos — circular por la Sierra del Gata, a 16.3 miles (26.2 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 51 minutes to complete, offering views of the Sierra de Gata. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Acebo from the Jevero natural pool — circular through Jálama, a difficult 31.7 miles (51.0 km) path through the Jálama area, passing a natural pool. Local mountain bikers also love the Sierra de Gata: Jálama Loop, a 46.5 miles (74.9 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Gata and Jálama, often completed in about 7 hours 29 minutes. Mountain biking around Hoyos is defined by rugged terrain, rolling hills, and extensive oak and cork oak forests. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Hoyos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Hoyos?
A: There are 13 mountain bike trails around Hoyos listed in this guide, catering to various skill levels. The wider Sierra de Gata region, where Hoyos is located, features an IMBA-approved MTB center with 10 distinct routes spanning 233 kilometers, designed for both mountain biking and all-terrain biking.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Hoyos?
A: The terrain around Hoyos is rugged and diverse, characterized by rolling hills, mountains like Mount Moncalvo (1055 m) and Pico Lameros (866 m), and extensive forests of oak and cork oak. You'll encounter varied surfaces, including both paved and unpaved sections, with routes ranging from moderate to difficult in elevation gain.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners around Hoyos?
A: While the trails listed in this guide are primarily moderate to difficult, the Sierra de Gata region's IMBA-approved MTB center offers routes designed for a range of skill levels. It's advisable to check the specific route details for easier sections or trails that might suit beginners within the broader network.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Hoyos area?
A: Yes, many routes around Hoyos are circular. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1250953529/trevejo-desde-hoyos-circular-por-la-sierra-del-gata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trevejo desde Hoyos — circular por la Sierra del Gata&lt;/a&gt; is a 26.2 km loop offering views of the Sierra de Gata. Another option is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251009325/acebo-from-the-jevero-natural-pool-circular-through-jalama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acebo from the Jevero natural pool — circular through Jálama&lt;/a&gt;, a 51.0 km path through the Jálama area.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints I can see while mountain biking near Hoyos?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You'll encounter lush, green valleys, abundant springs, and extensive forests of oak and cork oak. Routes often provide views of mountains like Mount Moncalvo and Pico Lameros. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251009325/acebo-from-the-jevero-natural-pool-circular-through-jalama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acebo from the Jevero natural pool — circular through Jálama&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, passes by a natural pool, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1250953529/trevejo-desde-hoyos-circular-por-la-sierra-del-gata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trevejo desde Hoyos — circular por la Sierra del Gata&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views of the Sierra del Gata.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or villages to explore along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the area is dotted with charming villages and historical sites. You can explore the traditional stone houses and picturesque streets of Hoyos itself, which is home to the 10th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5575765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish of Our Lady of the Good Man&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby settlements include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5690813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villasbuenas de Gata&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6050936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín de Trevejo&lt;/a&gt;, which offer glimpses into the region's rich history.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Hoyos?
A: The Sierra de Gata region generally offers good conditions for outdoor activities. While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, the presence of abundant water resources and lush landscapes suggests that spring and autumn might be particularly pleasant, avoiding the peak heat of summer. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain biking options or activities near Hoyos?
A: While the mountain bike trails in this guide are rated moderate to difficult, the broader Sierra de Gata region offers diverse outdoor pursuits beyond just challenging biking. Families might enjoy exploring the charming towns, bird-watching, or visiting the natural swimming pool located a few kilometers from Hoyos, which provides clean, clear waters and ample shade.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Hoyos?
A: Specific parking information for individual trailheads is not detailed in this guide. However, as Hoyos is a village and the administrative capital of the Gata region, you can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the village. It's recommended to check local signage or inquire locally upon arrival for the most convenient parking spots near your chosen starting point.

Q: Are there any cafes or amenities available along the mountain bike routes?
A: The mountain bike routes often connect or pass through charming towns and villages in the Sierra de Gata. Hoyos itself offers a warm and hospitable atmosphere with amenities. While specific cafes on the trails are not listed, you can expect to find places for refreshments and supplies in the villages you pass through, such as Hoyos, Villasbuenas de Gata, or San Martín de Trevejo.

Q: What is a challenging mountain bike route for experienced riders in the area?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1252070202/sierra-de-gata-jalama-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Gata: Jálama Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 74.9 km trail leading through the Sierra de Gata and Jálama, often completed in about 7 hours 29 minutes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19432679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo De Trevejo – San Martín de Trevejo loop from Cilleros&lt;/a&gt;, a 72.6 km route with significant elevation gain.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Hoyos?
A: The area is highly regarded by the komoot community, with over 50 mountain bikers having used komoot to explore Hoyos's varied terrain. Riders often praise the rugged and diverse landscape, the extensive forests of oak and cork oak, and the opportunity to explore charming towns and natural features within the Sierra de Gata region.
tips: A historic town dating back to Roman times, Cilleros is one of the largest towns in the Sierra de Gata. Located on the fertile agricultural plains of the Alagón River and the Sierras de Villuercas and Gata, the town is a natural gateway to adventures on both the Vía de la Plata and Vía Dalmacia.

To the north of Cáceres, more specifically in Sierra de Gata, is the Castle of Trevejo. This castle is famous for its history and for its typical architecture and its impressive location. There are incredible views of the town and the Sierra de Gata complex.

There is something better than jumping into the river on a warm summer day after a walk. The natural pools of the Jevero (Acebo) River are the perfect answer. With on-site parking, you can enjoy starting your adventure here and resting up afterward.

Hoyos is a highly regarded village in the Sierra de Gata mountains, renowned for its stately atmosphere, traditional architecture, and natural surroundings. It stands out for its tranquility, historical heritage, and mild microclimate, which for centuries attracted bishops and nobles to spend time there.

It's a perfect destination if you're looking for peace, nature, hiking, history, and authentic Extremaduran cuisine. It's also considered a village with a rich cultural legacy, reflected in its cobbled streets, palaces, and ancestral homes.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This challenging mountain bike route, covering nearly 75 km with significant elevation changes, is estimated to take around 7.5 hours of riding time. However, plan for a full day to account for stops, enjoying the views, and navigating the more technical sections.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Cilleros y Trevejo mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied and demanding. You'll encounter ancient historical paths, including sections of old Roman roads, dense forest areas with chestnut groves, and traditional rural landscapes. As you ascend to Pico Jálama, expect rocky sections with 'dura mucha piedra' (lots of stone) and low bushes. Some parts can be technical and rugged, requiring good bike handling skills.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is officially rated as difficult. The significant distance, elevation gain, and technical terrain, especially on the ascent to Pico Jálama, make it unsuitable for beginners. Riders should have a good level of fitness, experience with mountain biking, and strong technical skills.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular route?
faqs.answer: Since the route starts and ends in Hoyos, you can typically find parking within or near the village. The Historic Center of Hoyos is a good reference point for starting your ride. Look for available public parking areas in the village.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to ride this trail are generally in the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, especially in the lower elevations, while winters may bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher altitudes like Pico Jálama.

faqs.question: What historical or natural features will I encounter along the route?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in both history and nature. You'll pass through the charming village of Cilleros and near the historic Castillo De Trevejo. The trail also takes you by the Natural Pools of the Jevero River (Acebo) and the Historic Center of Hoyos. Expect ancient paths, dense chestnut groves, and panoramic views from Pico Jálama.

faqs.question: Is the Cilleros y Trevejo desde Hoyos mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact mountain bike trail are not detailed, the area is part of the Natura 2000 network. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially in areas with livestock or dense vegetation. Always practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for mountain biking in Sierra de Gata?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on public trails within the Sierra de Gata region. However, always respect private property, follow local signage, and adhere to any specific regulations that might be posted, especially within protected natural areas like the Natura 2000 zones the trail traverses.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The views are truly impressive, especially from the summit of Pico Jálama. You'll be treated to nearly 360-degree panoramas stretching across the province of Salamanca to the north, the Sierra de Gata to the east, Portugal to the west, and the province of Cáceres to the south. Along the way, enjoy traditional rural landscapes, terraced orchards, and dense forests.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several official trails. You'll ride along parts of the Camino Natural del Rio Ribera de Acebo, Camino del Payo, and the Cañada Real de Gata, among others. These intersections often follow ancient routes, adding to the historical character of the ride.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through or near several traditional villages like Hoyos, Cilleros, and Trevejo. These villages offer opportunities to find local cafes, restaurants, and various types of accommodation, from guesthouses to rural hotels. It's a great way to experience the local culture and cuisine.

waypoints.name: Natural Pools of the Jevero River (Acebo)
